description: This figure presents κ-phase microstructures in a 2Mn-8Al-0.2C (wt.%) low-density steel under different thermal histories. Panel (a) is an optical micrograph showing a nonlamellar eutectoid-like microstructure after cooling from 1400°C at 10°C/s and quenching at 870°C, where κ-phase and ferrite (α) precipitate at austenite (γ)/ferrite interfaces. Panels (b) and (c) are scanning electron microscopy (SEM) micrographs after deep etching, revealing three-dimensional morphologies. Figure 7b shows κ-phase growing along the long axis of austenite with complex blade-like precipitation. Figure 7c shows the microstructure after 15 min at 870°C, where isothermal treatment in the ferrite+κ domain promotes complete decomposition of austenite, leaving a cocoon-like κ-phase structure encasing the former austenite region. The comparison demonstrates the evolution from interfacial precipitation during continuous cooling to fully decomposed structures during isothermal holding.
